executive_summary.description: A high-level summary of the AI coding API market in April 2026, comparing the key strategic differences in offerings, pricing, and market positioning between US providers, Chinese providers, and aggregators.

comparative_overview_table.description: A summary table in string format comparing the most popular offerings from key US, Chinese, and aggregator providers across critical metrics like flagship model, price per million tokens (input/output), and standard monthly subscription cost.

provider.description: Name of the US-based provider (e.g., OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Perplexity).

subscription_tier.description: The name of the subscription tier (e.g., 'Pro', 'Free', 'Pay-as-you-go').

monthly_cost_usd.description: The equivalent monthly cost of the subscription tier in USD.

monthly_token_allocation.description: The number of tokens or usage credit allocated per month for this tier.

token_limits.description: Details on token limits, such as tokens-per-minute (TPM) or requests-per-minute (RPM).

model_name.description: The name of the specific AI model available in this offering (e.g., 'GPT-5.4', 'Claude 4.6').

input_price_per_million_tokens.description: The cost for 1 million input tokens for this model in USD.

output_price_per_million_tokens.description: The cost for 1 million output tokens for this model in USD.

context_window.description: The maximum context window size for the model (e.g., '1M tokens').

cli_access.description: Information on whether CLI tool access is available and any restrictions.

agent_compatibility.description: Details on compatibility with agents like OpenClaw/Hermes.

us_provider_offerings.description: Detailed breakdown of current AI coding API offerings from major US providers (OpenAI, Anthropic/Claude, Google, Perplexity). Each entry will include subscription tiers, monthly token allocations, token limits (per month/week/session), model availability (base vs. premium vs. lite), pricing per model, CLI tool access, agent compatibility (OpenClaw/Hermes), commercial restrictions, current base model, and any premium surcharges.

provider.description: Name of the Chinese provider (e.g., Qwen, MiniMax, Zhipu).

parent_company.description: Parent company of the provider (e.g., Alibaba, Baidu, ByteDance).

subscription_tier.description: The name of the subscription tier (e.g., 'Pro', 'Free', 'Pay-as-you-go').

monthly_cost_usd.description: The equivalent monthly cost of the subscription tier in USD.

monthly_token_allocation.description: The number of tokens or usage credit allocated per month for this tier.

token_limits.description: Details on token limits, such as tokens-per-minute (TPM) or requests-per-minute (RPM).

model_name.description: The name of the specific AI model available in this offering (e.g., 'Qwen3.6-Plus', 'GLM-5').

input_price_per_million_tokens.description: The cost for 1 million input tokens for this model in USD.

output_price_per_million_tokens.description: The cost for 1 million output tokens for this model in USD.

context_window.description: The maximum context window size for the model (e.g., '128k tokens').

cli_access.description: Information on whether CLI tool access is available and any restrictions.

agent_compatibility.description: Details on compatibility with agents like OpenClaw/Hermes.

chinese_provider_offerings.description: Detailed breakdown of current AI coding API offerings from major Chinese providers (Qwen/Alibaba, MiniMax, z.ai/Zhipu/GLM, Kimi/Moonshot, DeepSeek, Xiaomi/Mimo, Baidu/Ernie, ByteDance, StepFun). Each entry will include subscription tiers, monthly token allocations, token limits, model availability, pricing per model, CLI tool access, agent compatibility, commercial restrictions, current base model, and premium surcharges.

provider.description: Name of the aggregator provider (e.g., OpenRouter).

subscription_tier.description: The name of the subscription tier (e.g., 'Free Tier', 'Pay-as-you-go').

monthly_cost_usd.description: The equivalent monthly cost of the subscription tier in USD.

token_limits.description: Details on token limits imposed by the aggregator, such as requests per day or per minute.

pricing_model.description: Description of the aggregator's pricing model (e.g., 'Pass-through pricing with a small fee').

supported_models_summary.description: A summary of the range of models available through the aggregator.

cli_access.description: Information on how CLI tool access is managed through the aggregator.

agent_compatibility.description: Details on using agents like OpenClaw/Hermes through the aggregator.

aggregator_provider_offerings.description: Detailed breakdown of offerings from aggregator providers like OpenRouter. This will cover their subscription tiers, how they handle token allocations and limits for various models, their pricing structure, CLI tool access, and any specific rules or benefits they provide for agent compatibility.

subscription_tier_analysis.description: A comparative analysis of the common subscription tiers (e.g., free, pro at ~$20/month, enterprise) across all providers. This section details what users typically get for their money in terms of features, token amounts, and priority access.

token_limit_comparison.description: A detailed comparison of token limits imposed by different providers and their tiers. This includes tokens-per-minute (TPM), requests-per-minute (RPM), and any monthly, weekly, or session-based caps on token usage.

model_pricing_and_availability.description: An analysis of how providers structure pricing for different model classes. This compares the cost and availability of lite/flash models (for speed and cost), base/pro models (for general use), and premium/opus models (for maximum capability).

cli_and_agent_compatibility_report.description: A report on CLI tool access and agent framework compatibility. This details which providers offer dedicated CLI tools, which can be accessed via aggregators like OpenRouter, and any known compatibility or commercial restrictions related to using agents like OpenClaw or Hermes.

current_model_landscape_april_2026.description: An overview of the flagship and primary base models offered by each major provider as of April 2026. This includes model names (e.g., GPT-5.4, Claude 4.6, GLM-5) and their key features, such as context window size.

premium_model_surcharges.description: A summary of any surcharges or special pricing applied to premium models or for using extended context windows. This section clarifies any additional costs beyond the base token price.

market_trends_and_pricing_strategies.description: An analysis of the broader market trends observed as of April 2026. This includes the intense price competition, the increasing standardization of 1M+ token context windows, and the shift in pricing strategies, particularly in the Chinese market.

provider_name.description: The name of the provider offering the deal.

offer_type.description: The type of offer (e.g., 'Free Tier', 'Promotional Credit', 'New User Bonus').

description.description: A detailed description of the special offer or free tier.

value.description: The value of the offer, such as the number of free tokens or credit amount (e.g., '500,000 tokens', '$5 credit').

conditions.description: Any conditions, limits, or restrictions associated with the offer (e.g., 'For new users only', '50 requests/day limit').

pay_as_you_go_options.description: A description of the pay-as-you-go (PAYG) pricing models available from various providers. This section details the per-token costs for users who do not have a monthly subscription, providing an alternative to subscription-based plans.

data_sourcing_and_inference_notes.description: A note clarifying the sources of the data, acknowledging that where official pricing and limit data was unavailable, information was inferred from documentation, social media posts, and other secondary sources as per the user's request.
